MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Iaccarino for Doreen Phan (Petitioner).

The Court asks Counsel for his take on the material purposes of the Family Bypass Trust, the effect of the spendthrift clause and what it does to the material purposes. Mr. Iaccarino stated that he e-mailed his Client yesterday, that the value of the two trusts are $2,600,000.00, that it is well under the exemption amount, that the purpose of the trust was to avoid capital gains, and that the Mother is 89 years old and in an assisted living home. The Court spoke of and raised various scenarios. Mr. Iaccarino stated that the Daughter is in control, that she is the Trustee, that the Mother declined to be the Trustee, that as long as she remains competent, she can change the Trustee and that the Petitioner and the Mother never intend to sell the property. The Court asks Counsel if he told his Client that she may be giving up her right to trust assets when she signed the consent. Mr. Iaccarino responded "yes", stated that he believes he had a conversation with her early on, but not recently, that he did not want more time to provide further briefing and that he would like to submit on what's been filed. The Petition to Terminate Trust is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.
